member.name: T:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject
member.summary: A base class for ScriptableObjects that are meant to be globally accessible, at edit-time and runtime. The way such objects are loaded is driven by usages of attributes.

member.name: F:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject._typesFailedToLoad
member.summary: Types that failed to load. Used to limit attempts until a reimport happens. Also, there's a nasty bug at least in some Unity versions (verified on 2021), where if there's a type of the same name in multiple assemblies, AssetDatabase.GetMainAssetPath may return the wrong one.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject.ClearTypesFailedToLoad
member.summary: Use to clear internal list marking specific type as unable to load. Types might be unable to load during bootstrap, so cleaning the list in some static initializer is a good way to ensure things can recover.

member.summary: Is this instance a global instance.

member.summary: Invoked when the instance is loaded as global.

member.summary: Invoked when the instance is unloaded as global.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.OnDisable
member.summary: If the current instance is global, unsets and calls

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.GlobalInternal
member.summary: A singleton instance-like property. Loads or returns the current global instance. Derived classes can package it in a property with a different name. Throws if loading an instance failed.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.IsGlobalLoadedInternal
member.summary: Returns true if a global instance is loaded. Compared to , it does not attempt to load an instance.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.TryGetGlobalInternal(`0@)
member.summary: Loads or returns the current global instance. Returns if loading an instance failed.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.GetGlobalAsyncInternal
member.summary: Loads or returns the current global instance asynchronously. Returns if loading an instance failed.

member.name: M:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject`1.UnloadGlobalInternal
member.summary: Unloads the global instance if it is loaded.
member.returns: if an instance was unloaded

member.name: T:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectSourceAttribute
member.summary:
Base class for all attributes that can be used to load . Attributes need to be registered at the assembly level. For instance, this snippet is used to register a default loader, that attempts to load from Resources based on :
[assembly: Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResource(typeof(Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObject), Order = 2000, AllowFallback = true)]

member.param: Type or the base type of that this loader supports.

member.summary: Type or the base type of that this loader supports.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectSourceAttribute.Order
member.summary: Order in which this loader will be executed. Lower values are executed first.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectSourceAttribute.AllowEditMode
member.summary: Can this loader be used in edit mode.

member.name: P:Fusion.FusionGlobalScriptableObjectSourceAttribute.AllowFallback
member.summary: Does this loader allow fallback to the next loader?

member.param: The requested type

member.name: T:Unity.IL2CPP.CompilerServices.Option
member.summary: The code generation options available for IL to C++ conversion. Enable or disabled these with caution.

member.name: F:Unity.IL2CPP.CompilerServices.Option.NullChecks
member.summary:
Enable or disable code generation for null checks.
Global null check support is enabled by default when il2cpp.exe is launched from the Unity editor.
Disabling this will prevent NullReferenceException exceptions from being thrown in generated code. In *most* cases, code that dereferences a null pointer will crash then. Sometimes the point where the crash happens is later than the location where the null reference check would have been emitted though.

member.name: F:Unity.IL2CPP.CompilerServices.Option.ArrayBoundsChecks
member.summary:
Enable or disable code generation for array bounds checks.
Global array bounds check support is enabled by default when il2cpp.exe is launched from the Unity editor.
Disabling this will prevent IndexOutOfRangeException exceptions from being thrown in generated code. This will allow reading and writing to memory outside of the bounds of an array without any runtime checks. Disable this check with extreme caution.

member.name: F:Unity.IL2CPP.CompilerServices.Option.DivideByZeroChecks
member.summary:
Enable or disable code generation for divide by zero checks.
Global divide by zero check support is disabled by default when il2cpp.exe is launched from the Unity editor.
Enabling this will cause DivideByZeroException exceptions to be thrown in generated code. Most code doesn't need to handle this exception, so it is probably safe to leave it disabled.
